decisions.reasoning: IEA May 2026 report shows global oil demand expected to contract 2.4 mb/d YoY in Q2 and 420 kb/d for full year 2026. Price chart shows clear downtrend from $63.45 (Mar 30) to $56.85 today. US-Iran deal negotiations on reopening the Strait of Hormuz — if successful — would further increase supply and weigh on energy prices. With demand falling and supply-side risk to the downside on prices, holding energy ETF has no near-term upside thesis. Exited to rotate capital into stronger opportunity.

sources.headline: Oil Market Report May 2026 - demand contraction 2.4 mb/d y-o-y in Q2, oil prices swung $144 to sub-$100 on Iran deal signals
Stock Market Today: Russell 2000, Nasdaq jump 1% despite rocky weekend in Iran, Ukraine conflicts
S&P 500 is little changed as cybersecurity stocks pull back

decisions.invalidation: US-Iran deal collapses, Strait of Hormuz remains closed, oil spikes back above $120; would re-enter XLE on demand recovery signal

decisions.reasoning: SMA signal flipped to flat (fast SMA 416.52 crossed below slow SMA 417.70) — first negative crossover signal in portfolio universe. Near-term headwinds confirmed: record $37.5B capex in Q2 FY2026 with concerns about diminishing returns and free cash flow pressure. Trimmed from 5 to 3 shares to free capital for AAPL rotation; kept 3 shares as analysts hold $560 12-month target (34% upside) on Azure + Copilot thesis and AI capex risk seen as largely priced in.

sources.headline: Microsoft: The AI Capex Risk Looks Mostly Priced In
Is Microsoft Stock Vulnerable to Rising Capex Pressure From AI Spending
Microsoft Stock (MSFT) in May 2026: Profit, Loss, or Stagnation — near-term pressure from high capex and OpenAI losses

decisions.invalidation: Azure growth re-accelerates, capex-to-revenue efficiency improves, or SMA flips back to long signal; would add back to 5 shares

decisions.reasoning: Apple reported Q2 FY2026 on Apr 30: revenue $111.18B (+17% YoY, beat $109.66B est), EPS $2.01 (beat $1.95 est). June-quarter guidance 14-17% growth — more than double analyst expectation of 9.5%. Board authorized additional $100B share buyback + 4% dividend raise. Google/Gemini AI partnership announced for Siri, addressing prior AI strategy concerns. SMA signal is firmly long (fast $303 vs slow $285, price $311). Next earnings July 30 — no near-term earnings risk for 9 weeks. Price is in a steady uptrend ($271 post-earnings to $312 today, no sharp one-day gaps).

sources.headline: Apple Q2 2026 earnings report — 17% revenue growth, $111.18B revenue beats estimates
Apple (AAPL) Earnings Date and Reports 2026 — next report July 30
AAPL Q2 2026 Earnings — better-than-expected June quarter revenue forecast, $100B buyback authorized

decisions.invalidation: iPhone unit sales disappoint in June quarter pre-announcement, Google/Gemini integration delayed or cancelled, broader tech selloff breaks price below $290 (10% below entry)

decisions.reasoning: Strong Q1 FY2027 earnings reported May 20: $81.6B revenue (beat $78.8B est, +85% YoY), EPS $1.87 (beat $1.76). Q2 guidance $91B vs $87.2B consensus. 62 analysts Strong Buy, avg target $295.69 (+38% from current). Chip sector rally today per TheStreet. CANNOT exit within 3-day window (2 shares added yesterday May 26). Thesis intact; holding full 21 shares.

decisions.reasoning: Long SMA signal (fast $743.25 vs slow $726.31, price $750.61). S&P 500 up 9.2% YTD driven by forward earnings growth. Broad macro backdrop positive with tech/semiconductor leadership. No catalyst to exit.

decisions.invalidation: Forward earnings estimates reverse, recession indicators trigger, price breaks below 30-day SMA

summary: First session: rotated out of XLE (bearish energy macro/Iran deal) and trimmed MSFT (flat SMA crossover/capex risk); initiated AAPL on Q2 earnings beat + buyback + AI partnership; held NVDA/SPY on strong fundamentals
open_theses: NVDA: record-beat Q1 earnings, Q2 guide $91B, data center 87% of revenue, chip-sector rally — 3-day hold window lifts May 29
AAPL: post-earnings momentum, 14-17% June quarter guidance vs 9.5% consensus, $100B buyback backstop
MSFT: reduced to 3 shares, watching for SMA recovery and capex narrative improvement before adding back
SPY: long signal, broad macro positive, monitoring for any break below 30-day SMA as macro risk trigger
